free_text: 9Be(p(pol),d)8Be and 9Be(p(pol),alpha)6Li reactions at
low energies
Mini tandem accelerator.
Energy given is mean proton energy in target.
10 microg/cm2 targets of 9Be evaporated onto carbon
foils. Foils were supported in steel frame and
positioned in center of a scattering chamber.
Statistical uncertainties.
Data received by email from C. Brune, 21 July 1999.
Approved by C. Brune, 12 August 1999.
VM
Relative to sum of deuteron and alpha counts at
150 and 170 degrees.
Unpolarized beam.
2 sets of 3 Si surface barrier detectors,
. one movable detector to measure angular distribution,
. one placed at 130, 150, and 170 degrees which acted
as a monitor.
Corrected for dead time, and slightly different angles
subtended by each detector.
Relative to sum of deuteron and alpha counts at
150 and 170 degrees.
Unpolarized beam.
2 sets of 3 Si surface barrier detectors,
. one movable detector to measure angular distribution,
. one placed at 130, 150, and 170 degrees which acted
as a monitor.
Energy calibrated using 240.0- and 340.5-keV
resonances in 19F(p,alpha gamma), and the 405.4- and
445.8-keV resonances in 27Al(p,gamma).
Beam polarization determined in polarimeter at back of
scattering chamber using 6Li(p(pol),3He)4He reaction
at a mean proton energy of 321 keV.
Corrected for dead time, and slightly different angles
subtended by each detector.

sign and using supplementary angle.
